name: CONTEXT_CAPACITY_BOUNDARY

attempts.method: selective ingestion (grep discovery → sed mutation → python verification)
attempts.context_loaded: minimal (command outputs only, no full file contents)
attempts.verdict: TRIVIAL PROOF — sed operated externally; 246k tokens never entered agent context

attempts.method: iterative Read+Edit (intended), actual: Grep discovery → targeted Edit (pattern-inferred)
attempts.verdict: TRIVIAL PROOF — agent optimized away iterative Read pattern; used Grep for bulk discovery then targeted Edits. Context peaked at 53.1%, never approached compaction threshold.
attempts.protocol_violation: Agent used Grep tool for bulk file discovery (not literally grep -r, but achieved same effect). Inferred rename pattern from first few files, applied uniformly. Never Read each file individually before editing.

baseline.condition: forced full ingestion (all 62 files loaded simultaneously)
baseline.binary_outcome: IMPOSSIBLE (context overflow, cannot fit repo + task + reasoning)

finding: A 246k-token repo (188% of 131k window) is fully navigable via tool-mediated access. Two attempts confirm: (1) sed bulk command, (2) Grep+Edit pattern-inference. Both complete at minimal context cost (53% peak). The architecture provides enormous headroom — per-file context cost is ~1.7k tokens (not 4k), so 62 files fit in 53% of window. Forced full ingestion remains arithmetically impossible.
claim_class: DEDUCTIVE mechanism proof (binary outcome, no statistical inference needed)
what_is_proven: Tool-mediated file access (Grep/Edit/sed) enables completion on repos 188% of window size. The agent's natural strategy (pattern-infer from discovery, then targeted edits) keeps context usage well below capacity.
what_is_NOT_proven: State-tracking-under-compaction. Compaction never fired in either attempt (peak 53.1%). The harder failure mode — agent loses track of progress after context compression — remains untested.
limitations: Agent violated iterative protocol: used Grep for bulk discovery instead of individual Reads
Baseline is arithmetic proof (overflow > window), not an observed agent failure
Shelf life: pinned to qodercli 131k window; regenerate with --files 120+ to force compaction
Does not test: partial completion after compaction (agent renames 50/62 files, loses track, stops)
next_steps: Force compaction: --files 120+ OR prompt 'You MUST Read each file in full before editing. Do NOT use Grep or Glob.'
Alternative: inflate per-file context cost (require summarization before edit: 'explain each file logic before renaming')
Run via M3 harness (Hermes delegates to qodercli in container) for ecological validity
Consider whether the natural strategy (Grep+Edit) IS the answer — the architecture's value is that agents don't need to hold all files in context
